name: Agent Evaluation and Benchmarking
description: Frameworks and metrics for measuring agent capabilities across task completion rate, efficiency, robustness, and safety. Unlike static NLP benchmarks, agent evaluation must account for multi-step trajectories, environment interaction costs, and failure recovery. Benchmark saturation is already a problem — top models cluster near ceiling on most published benchmarks within months of release.
contrarian_take: The agent benchmarking field has a Campbell's Law problem: as soon as a benchmark becomes widely used, labs optimize directly for it and the benchmark stops measuring general capability. Papers introducing new benchmarks are valuable only if they make contamination-resistant design explicit. Look for benchmarks with procedurally generated tasks, held-out environments, or human-in-the-loop evaluation components.
example_signals: AgentBench: Evaluating LLMs as Agents in Diverse Environments
GAIA: A Benchmark for General AI Assistants
Benchmark Contamination and Saturation in Large Language Model Evaluations
